name: Wolfe and Barber Acceptable Augmentation Calibration Extract
population_or_context: Automated scoring of student essay responses using simulated essays for data augmentation.
sample_size: 96 real student essays and 288 simulated essays generated from three prompt-engineering strategies.
methods: Extracted simulated essay generation, expert rating process, score-agreement analysis, score-distribution comparison, and realism judgments.
education_context: Student essay responses scored with a four-point rubric; grade band and prompt family are study-specific.
response_origin: Large-language-model simulated essays are paired with real student essays to support automated-scoring data augmentation research.
label_source: Expert raters assign scores and realism judgments to simulated essays; original real essays had professional ratings.
generation_model: Large language model; the public preprint text reviewed here does not name a specific model version.
generation_method: Three prompt-based methods generate simulated essays from real student essays.
prompt_strategy: 25 examples, Predict Next, and Sentence prompting strategies are compared.
evaluation_method: Human scoring agreement between original and simulated essays, score distribution comparisons, and expert realism ratings.
scorer_use: Acceptable as calibration and augmentation-suitability evidence before automated-scoring development; insufficient as direct synthetic-only validation of a scorer.
real_response_comparator: Real student essays are the comparator for simulated essay quality and score preservation.
reported_metrics: Exact and adjacent agreement, weighted kappa, score-distribution differences, and realism ratings across prompting methods.
limitations: Expert realism and score preservation do not by themselves establish downstream automated-scorer validity.
The result is essay-specific and prompt-method-specific.
